reviews.review_description: There is no offline mode and servers have been shut down. Way to go devs.
If you have a VR, you must have this. I haven't seen any guest hate it.

developer_response.body: Wow! That's a great compliment! Thank you for playing our game! Please enjoy what we prepared! Thank you.

reviews.review_description: So good to play with my kids

developer_response.body: Thank you for your comment. We hope your children and you have nice time.

reviews.review_title: A good game to play with your family
reviews.review_description: I've always looked for something to play with my children and this is it! Cute but serious minigames are fun to play and the concept is interesting. Recommended for dads like me.

reviews.review_title: My kids still playing this game
reviews.review_description: We didn't expect we are still playing this game. My kids love it so much.

developer_response.body: Thank you for playing and loving our game! We will try to create better games. Thank you so much!

reviews.review_description: With the update, the game got sweeter. I like the new moods of the house!

I will eventually cover this game on my YouTube channel but felt the need to warn people ahead of time so they don't waste their money. (I paid my own money for this game)

What is Crazy World... well its an Oculus Go game that was free converted into a $20 app lab game. The graphics look blurry on quest 2 and there were brief moments of stuttering perhaps from recording but they were definitely there. You get a small hub area that admittedly looks alright minus the blurriness and you can draw stuff that disappears almost immediately making it completely pointless of a function. 

You have a store to "randomly" unlock objects with the coins you make in game but the objects you unlock will say "2 days" or some period of time that the item (which are just skins) expire. There is no list to pick what you want and the whole store aspect is horrible.

But the games, there are 5 of them here. 

1. Shooting boxes - simple but fun, entertaining to play through once
2. Target Practice - use have a crossbow device to shoot a target, it moves in later rounds but is so easy and boring its not worth playing at all
3. Bowling - giant ball, and lane, looks silly, and ball rolls so slowly it goes off to the edge no matter what, this one is probably the worst of all the games
4. Tennis - it moves you to where the ball is and you hit it. No challenge of any kind, very little fun. Has multiplayer but no one else bought this game so no one online.
5. Showdown - looks to be a quick draw thing but its multiplayer only and again... no one is playing so no multiplayer. 

Overall I would say this is a $4 game being massively over priced. I would suggest staying far away from this as even the youngest of players will be bored of this in less then an hour.

Thanks for reading my review,
Lipnox VR

developer_response.body: Dear Lipnox VR, thank you for your elaboration about our game. Even if we have something disappointing for you, for now, we will keep listening and try to improve it. Actually, we are planning to update the game continuously, so please look forward to it. Thank you.

reviews.review_description: Even better with fishing and stuff...liked it alot.

developer_response.body: Thank you for appreciating our update! Please enjoy playing CWVR!

reviews.review_title: Not extremely fun, but not that bad...
reviews.review_description: Mini games such as tennis or bowling, I think these refer to Wii sports, are not bad. It seems I can meet people at... lounge? or something, but I haven't met someone yet. The game has many contents, but shallow now. Need more updates, and users. I will recommend to play this game for VR/Quest beginners.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your interest in our game. Actually, Crazy World VR is a game that evolves. We will keep updating the game, so please look forward to our news!
